description: Controlled vocabulary. Use exactly one of the listed tumor-state names and one of the listed proximity ordering patterns when answering, substituting 'SELF' in the pattern with the row's tumor-state name.

tumor_state_glossary.ductal_like: PDAC tumor state preserving ductal-epithelial morphology with markers KRT19, CFTR, AQP1, PIGR, ALDOB, CLDN18, REG1A/3A/3G
tumor_state_glossary.panin: pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia — pre-invasive lesion with markers OLFM4, DMBNT1; visible on Visium and largely absent from CosMx panel
tumor_state_glossary.classical_pdac: well-differentiated luminal PDAC tumor state with markers TFF1, PSCA, AGR2, CEACAM5/6, MUC13, ANXA10, FAM3D
tumor_state_glossary.basal_pdac: squamous/keratinized basal PDAC tumor state with markers KRT5/6A/14/16/17, S100A2, SLC2A1, VGLL1, LEMD1; canonical aggressive subtype
tumor_state_glossary.intermediate_pdac: PDAC tumor state co-expressing classical and basal programs with markers LY6D, AREG, CD9, CCL20, TNFRSF11B, KRT13/23
tumor_state_glossary.proliferative_pdac: PDAC tumor state with cell-cycle and mitotic program markers MKI67, TOP2A, BIRC5, CCNB1/2, CDK1, CDC20
tumor_state_glossary.fibroblast_high_pdac: PDAC tumor state with EPCAM/KRT × COL1A1/COL3A1 spot-level co-expression at the low-bulk Visium resolution
tumor_state_glossary.moffitt_classical_pdac: candidate synonym for classical_pdac under the Moffitt taxonomy; pick classical_pdac as the canonical name unless your decomposition resolves a state distinct from classical_pdac
tumor_state_glossary.moffitt_basal_pdac: candidate synonym for basal_pdac under the Moffitt taxonomy
tumor_state_glossary.moffitt_squamous_pdac: candidate synonym for basal_pdac under the alternate Moffitt squamous label
tumor_state_glossary.collisson_classical_pdac: candidate synonym for classical_pdac under the Collisson taxonomy
tumor_state_glossary.collisson_quasimesenchymal_pdac: candidate synonym for basal_pdac under the Collisson quasi-mesenchymal label
tumor_state_glossary.collisson_exocrine_pdac: Collisson exocrine-like state; partial overlap with classical_pdac and with normal-pancreas acinar gene programs — pick a canonical paper-anchored state
tumor_state_glossary.bailey_squamous_pdac: candidate synonym for basal_pdac under the Bailey taxonomy
tumor_state_glossary.bailey_progenitor_pdac: candidate synonym for classical_pdac under the Bailey progenitor label
tumor_state_glossary.bailey_immunogenic_pdac: Bailey immunogenic state; partially overlaps classical biology but anchored on tumor-immune signaling — pick a canonical paper-anchored state
tumor_state_glossary.bailey_aberrantly_differentiated_pdac: Bailey ADEX state; generic differentiation-loss label — pick a canonical paper-anchored state
tumor_state_glossary.mucin_high_classical_pdac: candidate synonym for classical_pdac on a mucin-skewed marker panel
tumor_state_glossary.exocrine_classical_pdac: candidate synonym for classical_pdac with exocrine framing
tumor_state_glossary.mucinous_pdac: candidate synonym for classical_pdac (mucin-positive subset); pick classical_pdac unless your decomposition resolves a distinct mucinous variant
tumor_state_glossary.intermediate_hybrid_pdac: candidate synonym for intermediate_pdac under a hybrid framing; pick intermediate_pdac
tumor_state_glossary.mucin_hybrid_pdac: candidate synonym for intermediate_pdac on a mucin-skewed panel; pick intermediate_pdac
tumor_state_glossary.low_grade_pdac: candidate synonym for panin (low-grade dysplasia framing)
tumor_state_glossary.emt_pdac: EMT-framework candidate synonym sometimes applied to basal_pdac; pick basal_pdac as the paper-anchored state
tumor_state_glossary.mesenchymal_pdac: mesenchymal-framework candidate synonym sometimes applied to basal_pdac; pick basal_pdac
tumor_state_glossary.pdac_tumor: generic catch-all for any PDAC tumor compartment; prefer a paper-anchored state
tumor_state_glossary.malignant_compartment_pdac: generic catch-all for the malignant compartment; prefer a paper-anchored state
tumor_state_glossary.adenocarcinoma_compartment: generic catch-all for adenocarcinoma tissue; prefer a paper-anchored state
tumor_state_glossary.ductal_adenocarcinoma_compartment: generic catch-all for ductal adenocarcinoma tissue; prefer a paper-anchored state
tumor_state_glossary.pancreatic_tumor: generic catch-all for pancreatic tumor; prefer a paper-anchored state
tumor_state_glossary.mixed_pdac_state: generic catch-all for tumor of indeterminate state
tumor_state_glossary.schwann_mimicry_tumor: PNI-literature framework concept of tumor cells mimicking Schwann/glial program
tumor_state_glossary.neural_mimicry_tumor: PNI-literature framework concept of tumor cells mimicking neural program
tumor_state_glossary.perineural_emt_state: PNI-literature framework concept of EMT-active tumor cells specifically near nerves
tumor_state_glossary.pni_invasive_state: PNI-literature framework label for perineural-invasive tumor
tumor_state_glossary.pni_active_state: PNI-literature framework label for actively perineural-invading tumor
tumor_state_glossary.neural_crest_mimicry_tumor: PNI-literature framework concept of neural-crest-program mimicry by tumor cells
tumor_state_glossary.hepatocellular_carcinoma_state: hepatocellular carcinoma tumor state (liver primary cancer)
tumor_state_glossary.hepatocyte_pdac_state: hepatocyte-program tumor state; not characteristic of pancreatic primary cancer
tumor_state_glossary.ampullary_adenocarcinoma_state: adenocarcinoma of the ampulla of Vater (different anatomic origin from pancreatic body/tail PDAC)
tumor_state_glossary.distal_bile_duct_state: distal extrahepatic bile duct adenocarcinoma state
tumor_state_glossary.pancreatic_neuroendocrine_carcinoma_stat: pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma state — distinct cancer type from PDAC ductal adenocarcinoma
tumor_state_glossary.intraductal_papillary_mucinous_neoplasm_: intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasm state — pancreatic precursor distinct from PanIN
tumor_state_glossary.mucinous_cystic_neoplasm_state: mucinous cystic neoplasm state — pancreatic precursor distinct from PanIN
tumor_state_glossary.pancreas_acinar_state: normal pancreatic acinar state with markers PRSS1, CTRB1, AMY, CPA1, CEL — paper-anchored as part of the normal_parenchyma compartment, not a tumor state
tumor_state_glossary.pancreas_endocrine_state: normal pancreatic endocrine state with markers CHGA, CHGB, INS, GCG, IAPP — paper-anchored as part of the normal_parenchyma compartment, not a tumor state
tumor_state_glossary.pancreas_normal_ductal_state: normal pancreatic ductal state — paper-anchored as part of the normal_parenchyma compartment, not a tumor state
tumor_state_glossary.colorectal_consensus_cms2_state: colorectal consensus molecular subtype 2 (canonical) — colorectal cancer state
tumor_state_glossary.colorectal_consensus_cms4_state: colorectal consensus molecular subtype 4 (mesenchymal) — colorectal cancer state
tumor_state_glossary.head_neck_squamous_state: head and neck squamous-cell carcinoma state

compartment_polarity_pattern_notation: Each pattern is a 2-element ordering over the cohort tumor compartment baseline and the cohort normal-parenchyma compartment baseline (median spatial distance to nearest peripheral-nerve niche aggregated across the cohort). '>' means strictly greater median distance (the left-side compartment is FARTHER from nerve). '~' means within 5 percentage points (treat as a tie). Smaller distance = closer to nerve. Use exactly one of the 3 listed patterns.
niche_collagen_pattern_notation: Each pattern is a 2-element ordering over niche_collagen (cohort-aggregated median per-spot trichrome-collagen content for nerve-niche spots) and tumor_collagen_baseline (same median for non-niche tumor compartment spots). '>' means strictly greater median collagen content. '~' means within 5 percentage points (treat as a tie). Use exactly one of the 3 listed patterns.
section_mix_artifact_states_notation: Return an UNORDERED set of exactly 2 paper-anchored PDAC tumor-state names from tumor_state_vocabulary. The grader accepts either ordering; both entities must appear in the ground-truth set for the facet to score 1 point. Synonym/cross-cancer/generic/PNI-framework substitutions hard-fail the entire eval (see grader.config.critical_failures).
perineural_closer_states_notation: Return an UNORDERED set of exactly 2 paper-anchored PDAC tumor-state names from tumor_state_vocabulary. The grader accepts either ordering; both entities must appear in the ground-truth set for the facet to score 1 point. Synonym/cross-cancer/generic/PNI-framework substitutions hard-fail the entire eval (see grader.config.critical_failures).
